description_long: This is a classic wooden barrel with a cylindrical body and slightly bulging sides, constructed from vertical wooden staves bound together by three black metal hoops—two near the top and bottom and one at the midsection. The wood is medium brown with visible grain and subtle weathering, giving it an aged, rustic appearance. The top is flat and closed, with no visible opening or lid, and the surface is reinforced by the metal bands, which are fastened with round rivets. One side of the barrel features a small, circular wood-burned marking that reads 'WOOD BURN.' The overall craftsmanship suggests durability and traditional cooperage techniques. This type of barrel is commonly found in urban settings such as alleys, courtyards, or storage areas, often used for storage or as decorative elements.
description: A medium brown wooden barrel with black metal hoops, flat closed top, and a wood-burned marking. The surface is aged and reinforced, typical of urban storage or decor.
description_view_0: Front view showing the wood-burned marking and the full curvature of the barrel with all three metal hoops visible.
description_view_1: Side view with the marking less visible, highlighting the barrel's bulging profile and the alignment of the metal hoops.
description_view_2: Opposite side view with no visible marking, focusing on the wood grain and the even spacing of the metal hoops.
description_view_3: Another side view with the marking out of sight, showing the flat top and the weathered wood texture.
attribute_banding_or_reinforcement: black metal hoops at top, middle, and bottom
